title: BIOL 105 - Principles of Biology
description: Fundamental processes of life. Structure and function of cells, genetics, evolution, and ecology.

title: CHEM 109 - General Chemistry I
description: Fundamental concepts: chemical foundations, stoichiometry, chemical reactions, gases, thermochemistry, atomic theory, bonding, liquids, solutions. For students in science, engineering, and related majors.

title: CHEM 110 - General Chemistry II
description: Fundamental concepts: kinetics; equilibrium; acids and bases; acid-base, solubility, and complex ion equilibria; entropy and free energy; electrochemistry; qualitative analysis. For students in science, engineering, and related majors.

title: GEOL 109 - General Geology
description: Physical geology. Origin and constitution of the earth, internal and external processes that determine crustal and surficial features, and methods in investigating and interpreting earth history.

title: OCN 109 - General Oceanography
description: Extent of the oceans; chemical nature of sea water; causes/effects of currents, tides, and waves; animal and plant life in the sea; features of the ocean floor.

title: OCN 109L - General Oceanography Laboratory
description: Introductory laboratory activities to develop understanding of fundamental concepts from geological, chemical, physical, and biological oceanography and the inter-relationship between those concepts.

title: OCN 260 - Sampling Techniques and Field Studies
description: Introductory course for majors. Biological, chemical, geological, and physical oceanographic methods of sampling and analysis. Shipboard procedures and navigation.

title: OCN 310 - Biological Oceanography
description: Physical, chemical, and biological factors characterizing the marine environment, including factors controlling plant and animal populations. Methods of sampling identification and analysis.

title: OCN 320 - Physical Oceanography
description: Physical properties and processes in seas: theory of distribution of variables; current determination; waves and tides.

title: OCN 330 - Chemical Oceanography
description: Composition of seawater. Distribution and cycling of important major and minor chemical species throughout the oceans. Marine analytical chemistry.

title: OCN 340 - Geological Oceanography
description: Classification/origin of major topographic features on ocean floor. First order plate tectonic theory. Recent marine sediments and sedimentary processes.

title: OCN 370 - Library Research and Report Writing Seminar
description: Access oceanographic literature and write reports.

title: OCN 420 - Oceans and Climate
description: Examines the role that oceans play in mediating global climate. Detailed exploration of ocean carbon cycle, consequences of climate change on ocean ecosystems, ocean-related climate feedback loops, and predictions of oceans of the future.

title: OCN 485 - Undergraduate Seminar
description: Study literature to prepare oral scientific reports.

title: OCN 495W - Field Cruise I
description: Develop a research proposal. Conduct research on extended cruise. Use oceanographic techniques and theory on-board ship.

title: OCN 496 - Field Cruise II
description: Process oceanographic samples and analyze research data. Prepare a final cruise report.

title: MATH 109 - Calculus I
description: Limits, continuity, derivatives, integrals, and their applications.

title: MATH 110 - Calculus II
description: Logarithmic and exponential functions, inverse trigonometric functions, techniques of integration, infinite sequences and series, conic sections, polar coordinates.

title: MATH 210 - Calculus III
description: Vectors; parametric equations; 3-dimensional analytic geometry; vector- valued functions; partial derivatives; multiple integrals; introduction to line integrals.

title: PHYX 109 - General Physics A: Mechanics
description: Calculus-based, for science/engineering students.

title: PHYX 210 - General Physics B: Thermodynamics, Waves and Optics
description: Calculus-based, for science/ engineering students.

title: MATH 105 - Calculus for the Biological Sciences and Natural Resources
description: Differential and integral calculus. Apply to biological sciences, including exponential growth and decay.

title: MATH 215 - Multivariate Calculus for the Biological Sciences and Natural Resources
description: Differential equations, partial derivatives, double integrals, and curve fitting techniques; vectors; applications.

title: PHYX 106 - College Physics: Mechanics and Heat
description: Non-Calculus, for science majors. Mechanics, fluids, heat, sound.

title: PHYX 107 - College Physics: Electromagnetism and Modern Physics
description: Non-Calculus, for science majors. Geometric optics, electricity, magnetism, electromagnetic waves, AC circuits, physical optics, relativity.

title: STAT 109 - Introductory Biostatistics
description: Descriptive statistics, probability, random variables, discrete and continuous distributions, confidence intervals, contingency tests, regression and correlation, tests of hypothesis, analysis of variance. Emphasis: methods and applications used in the biological and natural resource sciences.
